steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: ”Stratigraphy” and “the analysis of Earth's sediment layers” form an appositive pair—one noun phrase that renames or clarifies another.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for appositives
steps.content:
An em dash is the standard punctuation for setting off an appositive.

Choice A correctly uses it to set off “the analysis of Earth's sediment layers” as a clarification of “stratigraphy.” The remaining choices either ignore appositive conventions (D), lack closure (B), or misapply formal punctuation (C).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
